Title: A novel maize microRNA negatively regulates resistance to Fusarium verticillioides

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in maize kernels; induced in resistant line BT-1 and decreased in susceptible line N6 after Fusarium verticillioides inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2β-hydroxylation/deactivation of bioactive gibberellins, reducing active GA pool
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for degradation/inactivation of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis rosette leaves; maize tissues not directly quantified for GA in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant growth and development but increases susceptibility to Fusarium verticillioides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmGA2ox4 overexpression decreases bioactive GA function, causes dwarfism/delayed flowering/smaller leaves, and enhances resistance to Fusarium verticillioides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Target cleavage/suppression assays in maize protoplasts and tobacco; Arabidopsis overexpression; disease lesion and fungal biomass assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for bioactive GA deactivation, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium verticillioides infection; exogenous GA3; uniconazole treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmGA2ox4 is a direct target of zma-unmiR4. 2. Its transcript increases in resistant maize after Fusarium verticillioides infection but decreases in susceptible maize. 3. Heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis suppresses GA-mediated growth and enhances Fusarium verticillioides resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species is maize, but key functional disease assays for this gene were performed in Arabidopsis. The paper frames ZmGA2ox4 as part of a growth–defence balance module with zma-unmiR4.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning or derepressing ZmGA2ox4 could improve Fusarium verticillioides resistance and potentially lodging resistance, but excessive activation may penalize growth.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes impaired growth, dwarfism, delayed flowering, and reduced leaf size.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Down-regulated in zma-unmiR4 overexpression lines; induced functional context examined in leaves after Fusarium verticillioides infection
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deactivates bioactive gibberellins through 2-oxidation, lowering active GA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Bioactive gibberellins including GA1 and GA7-related active pool
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for degradation/inactivation of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes growth but negatively affects resistance to Fusarium verticillioides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtGA2ox7 overexpression lowers GA1 and GA7 accumulation, impairs growth, and enhances Fusarium verticillioides resistance; loss or repression has opposite effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient GUS assay; Arabidopsis overexpression and mutant phenotyping; metabolite profiling of GA1 and GA7; disease lesion, fungal biomass, DAB and trypan blue assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for bioactive GA deactivation, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium verticillioides infection; exogenous GA3; uniconazole treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGA2ox7 is a heterologous target of maize zma-unmiR4 in Arabidopsis. 2. Overexpression reduces bioactive GA accumulation and increases resistance to Fusarium verticillioides. 3. atga2ox7 mutant and zma-unmiR4 overexpressors show enhanced growth and increased susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtGA2ox8 was predicted but not supported as a functional target in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a proof-of-concept GA deactivation target for engineering disease resistance, though direct crop deployment would need mitigation of growth penalties.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarfism, delayed flowering, smaller leaves, darker green leaves with higher chlorophyll when overexpressed.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in maize kernels and various tissues; significantly repressed in resistant line BT-1 but induced in susceptible line N6 upon Fusarium verticillioides exposure
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fusarium verticillioides infection; likely promoter polymorphism-associated differential pathogen responsiveness between BT-1 and N6
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses ZmGA2ox4 and AtGA2ox7 transcript accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Bioactive gibberellin homeostasis via repression of GA-deactivating enzymes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant growth and development but compromises resistance to Fusarium verticillioides when elevated
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases bioactive GA accumulation, promotes plant growth, and reduces Fusarium verticillioides resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major switch balancing GA-mediated growth and Fusarium verticillioides resistance by suppressing GA2ox genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: miRNA identification by small RNA-seq, precursor RT-PCR, and RNA blot; target validation by maize protoplast YFP and tobacco GUS assays with mutant controls; Arabidopsis overexpression; GA quantification; pathogen assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium verticillioides infection; exogenous GA3; uniconazole treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. zma-unmiR4 is a novel Fusarium verticillioides-responsive maize miRNA. 2. It directly suppresses ZmGA2ox4 and heterologously suppresses AtGA2ox7. 3. Overexpression elevates GA accumulation, enhances growth, and compromises Fusarium verticillioides resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although discovered in maize kernels, most functional validation of downstream metabolic and disease effects was conducted in Arabidopsis. Promoter polymorphisms between BT-1 and N6 may explain differential induction.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning rather than strong overexpression may help balance resistance, growth, and lodging traits in maize; editing promoter or target site interactions may be more practical than constitutive overexpression.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced susceptibility to Fusarium verticillioides despite improved growth.
